text: SENATE: War powers resolution fails 50-49 — closest vote yet. Murkowski (R-AK), Collins (R-ME), Paul (R-KY) break with GOP. Senate has now rejected 7 attempts since March 2. Trump admin argues 60-day WPR clock expired under the ceasefire. Rubio calls WPR '100% unconstitutional.'
verificationNote: TIME, CBS News, The Hill, Newsweek, Fox News confirmed vote tally and defectors. Rubio quote confirmed by The Hill.

text: CONGRESSIONAL PRESSURE: Hegseth faces bipartisan grilling at House Armed Services Committee — WaPo reports 'intense frustration' from both parties over $29B war cost and lack of exit strategy. FDD overnight brief notes Soufan Center analysis: Trump-Xi summit faces high-profile issues including Iran, Taiwan, trade, AI oversight, and strategic minerals. Senate war powers vote 50-49 — unprecedented GOP defection.
verificationNote: Washington Post and PBS confirmed Hegseth grilling. Soufan Center intel brief confirmed. TIME and CBS News confirmed final vote count.

text: BEIJING DAY 1: Trump and Xi begin bilateral sessions. Iran, trade, Taiwan all on the table. Rubio and Hegseth both in delegation — dual-track pressure on Beijing over Hormuz and Iran oil purchases. PBS reports Trump and Xi 'appear intent on keeping Iran war from overshadowing summit' but CFR warns China holds upper hand. No joint statement expected today.
verificationNote: PBS NewsHour, CNN, and NPR confirmed both delegations entered formal bilateral sessions. CFR and CSIS analysis confirmed no joint statement expected.

text: ISRAEL-LEBANON TALKS: Third round of direct Israel-Lebanon talks begins in Washington today (May 14-15). Israeli delegation led by Ron Dermer, Lebanese by Simon Karam. Topics: border delineation, Lebanese sovereignty, Israeli withdrawal timeline, humanitarian corridors. Hezbollah FPV fiber-optic drones hit 41 IDF soldiers since ceasefire — unjammable system confirmed by WaPo. Israel still seeking counter-drone solution.
verificationNote: State Department official press release confirmed talks and delegation leads. Arab News, Xinhua, Jerusalem Post all confirmed May 14-15 date. Washington Post confirmed unjammable FPV fiber-optic drone system.

text: VANCE ON IRAN: VP Vance says US 'making progress' in Iran negotiations despite Trump rejection of Tehran's latest proposal. Key quote: 'The fundamental question is do we make enough progress that we satisfy the president's red line? He needs to feel confident we've put protections in place such that Iran will never have a nuclear weapon.' Al-Monitor and Haaretz both confirm Vance spoke with Kushner and Witkoff by phone before remarks.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor, Haaretz, Worldnews.com, and MarketScreener all confirmed Vance's May 13 statement. Quote verified by multiple pool reporters.

text: NUCLEAR ALARM: Energy Secretary Chris Wright tells Congress Iran is 'frighteningly close' to nuclear weapon — 'weeks away' from enriching one ton to weapons-grade. NBC reports Pentagon considering renaming resumed operations 'Sledgehammer' to reset 60-day WPR clock if ceasefire collapses. US Energy Secretary warning represents most explicit nuclear alarm from Trump cabinet to date.
verificationNote: CBS News live updates confirmed Wright's congressional testimony. NBC News confirmed Pentagon 'Sledgehammer' planning per unnamed official. CBS News, ABC News live updates both cited Wright's 'weeks away' figure.

text: KALLAS/EU: EU High Representative Kallas says Operation Aspides mandate could be extended to Strait of Hormuz if EU member states amend the operational plan — could escort merchant ships and conduct maritime mine clearance. EU Foreign Affairs Council meeting agreed to accelerate Strategic Partnership Agreements with all 6 Gulf Cooperation Countries. EU expanding Iran sanctions to include those obstructing freedom of navigation.
verificationNote: EUNews.it confirmed Kallas statements on Aspides and Gulf partnerships. Mehr News Agency confirmed EU backing diplomatic solution. EEAS official press conference confirmed.

text: IRAN INTERNAL: Pezeshkian government under pressure from hardliners — Iran International reports internal power struggle blocking compromise. Pezeshkian publicly insists 'dialogue does not mean surrender.' Iran military: Brig-Gen Akrami Nia maintains 'highest level of readiness, no room for retreat.' Adviser to Iran parliament speaker warns 90% uranium enrichment could resume if US attacks. Iran ready to 'repel new US attack' per CBS News.
verificationNote: Tribune India confirmed Pezeshkian 'no surrender' quote. Iran International confirmed internal power struggle. GlobalSecurity OPREP confirmed Akrami Nia readiness statement. CBS News confirmed readiness posture.
